twitter: Involuntary churn eats $374‑$936 of MRR every month for a $18.7K SaaS—yet most founders never track it. Stop letting failed payments be silent churn. https://saastools.corenk.com/articles/does-churn-prevention-saas-payments
linkedin: On a $18,730 MRR SaaS, 14 failed payments cost $1,247 in a single month—that's $374‑$936 lost every month to billing leaks alone. For bootstrapped founders under $50K MRR, that extra cash can mean the difference between extending runway or scrambling for new sales. Most churn dashboards blend voluntary and involuntary loss, so you’re celebrating retention wins while payment failures silently drain your revenue. Treat payment failures as a separate churn metric, track involuntary logo churn, and build a recovery playbook—dunning emails, smarter retries, card updater services. When you stop ignoring the billing layer, you turn a hidden leak into a steady cash flow boost. Want to see how fixing your payment stack can add up to $10K in ARR per quarter? https://saastools.corenk.com/articles/does-churn-prevention-saas-payments
title: Does Churn Prevention Ignore Your SaaS Payments? The Overlooked Leak That Bleeds MRR Monthly
